whatThisIs: Decode the UB-04 institutional-claim form, form locator by form locator. The UB-04 (CMS-1450) is the billing form used by hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, home health, hospice, and other institutional providers to bill Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial payers. Sophie Well covers every form locator from FL 1 (provider name and address) through FL 81 (code-code field), including the high-traffic spots: FL 4 (Type of Bill), FL 42-49 (revenue codes / HCPCS / dates / units / charges), FL 50-66 (payer and patient identifiers), FL 67-81 (diagnosis, procedure, and condition / occurrence / value code groups).
whenToUse: Use this when a remittance flags an institutional claim with a form-locator-specific error, when training a hospital biller on the difference between the CMS-1500 (professional) and UB-04 (institutional) world, or when verifying that an outside facility's UB-04 attached to a records request matches the encounter you have on file. The UB-04 maps directly to the electronic 837I transaction; understanding the FL numbers is the bridge between the paper form, the electronic claim, and the payer companion guide that connects them.
